title: Practice: Blue/Green Deployment
description:
Implement a Blue/Green deployment strategy with instant cutover capability for zero-downtime releases with immediate rollback safety.

**Focus**: Running two complete production environments side-by-side and switching traffic instantly via Service selector updates.

This exercise demonstrates the Blue/Green pattern where Blue (nginx:1.26) is the current production version and Green (nginx:1.27) is the new version. Both environments run simultaneously (6 total pods), but only one receives traffic based on the Service selector. The critical insight is that traffic switches instantly by updating the Service selector from `version: "1.26"` to `version: "1.27"` - no pod restarts required. This enables immediate rollback by simply reverting the selector. The pattern is ideal for high-stakes releases where instant rollback is more important than resource efficiency.
steps: Create Service that routes to Blue (selector: version=1.26)
Verify both Blue and Green deployments coexist (6 total pods)
Switch Service to Green by updating selector to version=1.27
Practice rollback by switching Service back to Blue
hints: Both deployments must have a common label (name: nginx)
Service selector controls which deployment receives traffic
Use 'kubectl patch svc' to update the Service selector
Both environments remain running - only traffic routing changes

editor_config.initial_code:
# Blue Deployment (Current Production - nginx 1.26)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-1.26
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: nginx
      version: "1.26"
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: nginx
        version: "1.26"
    spec:
      containers: 
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.26-alpine
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80

---
# Service (Initially routes to Blue)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: 
  name: nginx
  labels: 
    name: nginx
spec:
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
  selector: 
    name: nginx
    version: "1.26"  # TODO: Switch to "1.27" to route to Green
  type: NodePort

---
# Green Deployment (New Version - nginx 1.27)
# TODO: Create this deployment with version: "1.27" labels

_solution.code_solution:
# Blue Deployment (Current Production - nginx 1.26)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-1.26
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: nginx
      version: "1.26"
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: nginx
        version: "1.26"
    spec:
      containers: 
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.26-alpine
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80

---
# Service (Initially routes to Blue)
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata: 
  name: nginx
  labels: 
    name: nginx
spec:
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 80
  selector: 
    name: nginx
    version: "1.26"  # Will be updated to switch between blue/green
  type: NodePort

---
# Green Deployment (New Version - nginx 1.27)
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-1.27
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      name: nginx
      version: "1.27"
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        name: nginx
        version: "1.27"
    spec:
      containers: 
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.27-alpine
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 80
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
# Apply all manifests
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for both deployments
timeout=120
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  blue_ready=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-1.26 -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  green_ready=$(kubectl get deployment nginx-1.27 -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  
  if [ "$blue_ready" = "3" ] && [ "$green_ready" = "3" ]; then
    echo "✓ Both Blue and Green deployments ready (3 replicas each)"
    break
  fi
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done

# Verify total pod count
total_pods=$(kubectl get pods -l name=nginx --no-headers | wc -l)
if [ "$total_pods" -eq "6" ]; then
  echo "✓ Total of 6 pods running (3 Blue + 3 Green)"
  
  # Verify Service initially points to Blue
  svc_version=$(kubectl get svc nginx -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector.version}')
  if [ "$svc_version" = "1.26" ]; then
    echo "✓ Service initially routes to Blue (version 1.26)"
    
    # Switch to Green
    kubectl patch svc nginx -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"version":"1.27"}}}'
    sleep 5
    
    # Verify switch to Green
    svc_version=$(kubectl get svc nginx -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector.version}')
    if [ "$svc_version" = "1.27" ]; then
      echo "✓ Service switched to Green (version 1.27)"
      
      # Verify endpoints changed
      endpoint_count=$(kubectl get endpoints nginx -o jsonpath='{.subsets[*].addresses[*].ip}' | wc -w)
      if [ "$endpoint_count" -eq "3" ]; then
        echo "✓ Service has 3 endpoints (Green pods)"
        
        # Test rollback
        kubectl patch svc nginx -p '{"spec":{"selector":{"version":"1.26"}}}'
        sleep 3
        
        svc_version=$(kubectl get svc nginx -o jsonpath='{.spec.selector.version}')
        if [ "$svc_version" = "1.26" ]; then
          echo "✓ Rollback successful (back to Blue)"
          exit 0
        fi
      fi
    fi
  fi
fi

echo "✗ Blue/Green deployment validation failed"
kubectl get deployments -l name=nginx
kubectl get svc nginx -o yaml | grep -A 5 "selector:"
exit 1
